domain_hint: Conjugate heat transfer simulation of a three-pass serpentine turbine blade internal cooling channel (APEX-7 HPT, Phase 2B CDR review)
ambiguity_notes: (1) Slide 4 states medium mesh was used for production runs, but Slide 13 executive summary claims fine mesh was used for all reported results — direct numerical contradiction. (2) Slide 2 states spatially varying film effectiveness data was not incorporated because it was unavailable, but Slide 7 sensitivity table varies film effectiveness ±0.05 around 0.35 without documenting the basis for that bound, implying the team had more information than stated. (3) Slide 8 rig comparison uses Re_Dh = 18,000 in the simulation while the actual rig test report specifies Re_Dh = 16,400 — a 9.8% mismatch that is acknowledged in an internal memo but not corrected, undermining the validation claim. (4) Slide 5 cross-code comparison (OpenFOAM vs. Fluent) uses mismatched inlet turbulence intensities (5% vs. 3%) and is presented as a validation check, overstating the independence and rigor of that comparison.
